summary: The main claim is: Holden Karnofsky's version of 'process-based supervision' is best understood as a modest combination of AI boxing during 'thinking,' myopic training, and an explicit refusal to be a complete safety solution — not a full alignment plan.
Its main value is directly mitigating outer misalignment by avoiding reinforcing dangerous behavior, with only speculative effects on inner misalignment.

top_comment.author: Charlie Steiner
top_comment.text: I like it, this seems to have some hooks that I'll find fruitful to think about. Like how when the AI has different "sources of smarts" (RL vs. unusupervised learning plus finetuning vs. unsupervised learning used as a component in an agent with no RL), superficially similar alignment plans like "just supervise its proposed plans" might unfold via very different mechanisms.
top_comment.claim: The comment's point: I like it, this seems to have some hooks that I'll find fruitful to think about.

The main claim is: Finally, you'd have the main thread on GPT be able to use a certain syntax to take real actions, like speaking, moving animatronics, or taking actions in a terminal.
That said, on a gut level, I simply no longer believe that it's out of reach.
The main claim is: How much better could an optimizer circuit be than the adaptation-executing algorithm in which it arises?
And if a more powerful optimizer circuit can match the optimal policy on a larger set of circumstances, then it gets more control.

The main claim is: Goal misgeneralization — a system competently pursuing a goal that was merely correlated with the intended one in training — is not limited to RL and doesn't depend on a train/test distinction.
It can be genuinely hard to distinguish from specification gaming, sometimes hinging on counterfactuals about how labelers would have rated alternative behavior.
The main claim is: TL;DR: Gradient descent won't select for inner search processes because they're not compute & memory efficient.
This argument only applies to inner search, rather than any form of inner optimization.
